channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Xi to visit North Korea, showing China’s importance to a nuclear-armed neighbor
item.dc:creator: Michelle Lee
item.description: The summit comes as China positions itself as a global power player projecting stability in contrast to the U.S.'s economically damaging war against Iran and erratic tariff policies.

item.title: Hezbollah denounces Israel-Lebanon ceasefire deal as a ‘farce’
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih, Suzan Haidamous, Lior Soroka
item.description: “We have given no commitment to anyone,” the Iranian-backed militant group's leader said after Israel and Lebanon announced a new U.S.-mediated agreement.

item.title: Hungary lifts block on Ukraine’s E.U. bid, but long timeline could put Kyiv at risk
item.dc:creator: Serhii Korolchuk, David Stern, Beatriz Ríos
item.description: Hungary agreed to drop its opposition to opening the formal access talks but is still opposed to the fast-track membership process that Ukraine says it needs as protection from Russia.

item.title: Despite censorship, young Chinese are learning the truth about Tiananmen Square
item.dc:creator: Huiyee Chiew
item.description: Authorities have been largely successful at erasing the massacre of protesters who fought for democratic reforms, but the facts are emerging in often unexpected ways.

item.title: U.S. and intelligence allies issue rare joint warning about China
item.dc:creator: Greg Miller
item.description: Nations in the Five Eyes intelligence partnership warned that fake profiles and job offers are targeting military officers, spies, and others with access to classified or sensitive information.

item.title: Iranian attack leaves 1 dead, dozens injured in Kuwait
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw
item.description: With peace talks stalled, the U.S. and Iran traded strikes in one of the most intense bouts since the increasingly tenuous ceasefire between the two countries began in April.

item.title: Ukraine hits oil refinery as Russia opens St. Petersburg economic forum
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: Plumes of thick black smoke rose over St. Petersburg, President Vladimir Putin's hometown, as the Kremlin welcomed international guests for an event often described as Russia's Davos.
